Kwek Kwek Quiz Documentation

Last updated: March 2026 • Author: ljzh04

Welcome to Kwek Kwek Quiz

Kwek Kwek Quiz is a comprehensive quiz application that supports multiple question formats, AI-powered quiz generation, and collaborative features through peer-to-peer sharing.

Tip: You can use the app offline once loaded - all data is stored locally in your browser.

Getting Started

To get started with Kwek Kwek Quiz, you'll need to set up a few things depending on which features you want to use.

API Key Setup

To use the AI Quiz Generation feature, you'll need a Gemini API Key. You can obtain one from Google AI Studio.

Note: The API key is only required for AI-powered features. You can still use the app for manual quiz creation and taking without an API key.

Setup Steps

  1. Go to Google AI Studio
  2. Sign in with your Google account
  3. Create a new API key in the settings
  4. Copy the API key to your clipboard
  5. Paste it into the Gemini API Key field in the "Settings" page under "API Keys"

Creating Quizzes

Kwek Kwek Quiz offers multiple ways to create quizzes depending on your needs and technical expertise.

1. Manual JSON Creation

For developers or those familiar with JSON, you can directly create quizzes by writing valid JSON.

Best for: Precise control, complex quiz structures, or when you have existing quiz data.

2. AI-Powered Generation

Use the AI to generate quizzes from text descriptions. This requires an API key but is great for quick quiz creation.

Best for: Quick quiz creation, educational content, or when you need diverse question types.

3. Sample Quizzes

Load pre-built sample quizzes to see examples or use as templates for your own quizzes.

Best for: Learning the format, testing the app, or getting started quickly.

JSON Format

Quizzes are represented as JSON arrays containing question objects. Each question must have specific properties depending on its type.

Question Object Structure

Property Type Required Description
type string Yes Question type: "multiple-choice", "true-false", "fill-in-the-blank", or "identification"
question string Yes Markdown-formatted question text
correct any Yes Answer: index (MCQ), boolean (T/F), or string (other types)
options array Only for MCQ Array of option strings

Important: All strings must be properly escaped for JSON. Use double quotes inside strings and escape them with backslashes if needed.

Question Type Examples

Multiple Choice
JSON 
{
  "type": "multiple-choice",
  "question": "What is the capital of France?",
  "options": ["Berlin", "Madrid", "Paris", "Rome"],
  "correct": 2
}
True/False
JSON 
{
  "type": "true-false",
  "question": "The Earth is flat.",
  "correct": false
}
Fill-in-the-Blank
JSON 
{
  "type": "fill-in-the-blank",
  "question": "The chemical symbol for water is __.",
  "correct": "H2O"
}
Identification
JSON 
{
  "type": "identification",
  "question": "Who wrote 'To Kill a Mockingbird'?",
  "correct": "Harper Lee"
}

Complete Example

Here's a complete example showing all question types in a single quiz.

quiz.json 
[
  {
    "type": "multiple-choice",
    "question": "### What does this Python code do?\n```python\ndef add(a, b):\n    return a + b\n```",
    "options": ["Subtracts", "Adds", "Divides", "Multiplies"],
    "correct": 1
  },
  {
    "type": "true-false",
    "question": "**The Sun rises in the East.**",
    "correct": true
  },
  {
    "type": "fill-in-the-blank",
    "question": "The chemical symbol for **water** is H2_.",
    "correct": "O"
  },
  {
    "type": "identification",
    "question": "Who wrote 'To Kill a Mockingbird'?",
    "correct": "Harper Lee"
  }
]

Tip: You can use Markdown formatting in questions to make them more engaging. Supported elements include headers (###), bold (**text**), italics (*text*), and code blocks (```code```).

Taking Quizzes

Once a quiz is loaded, you can navigate through questions and submit answers using various methods.

Navigation

Control Action Description
Previous Click or press ← Go to previous question
Next Click or press → Go to next question
Home Click or press Home Go to first question
End Click or press End Go to last question

Tip: You can use keyboard shortcuts to navigate quickly through quizzes.

Answering Questions

Multiple Choice & True/False

Click on an option to select and submit your answer immediately.

Example: Click "Paris" to answer "What is the capital of France?"

Fill-in-the-Blank & Identification

Type your answer in the input field and press Enter or click Submit.

Example: Type "H2O" and press Enter to answer "The chemical symbol for water is __."

Tips & Best Practices

Tip

Use the "Format JSON" button to validate and pretty-print your JSON if you're having issues.

Note

The app saves your progress automatically, but you can also manually save quizzes to local storage.

Info

You can take quizzes offline once they're loaded - no internet connection needed.

Managing Quizzes

Kwek Kwek Quiz provides several ways to manage your quizzes for easy access and organization.

Saving Quizzes

How to Save a Quiz

  1. Enter a name for your quiz in the "Quiz Name" field
  2. Click the "Save" button (floppy disk icon)
  3. Your quiz will be saved to your browser's local storage
  4. The quiz will appear in the "Saved Quizzes" dropdown

Note: Saved quizzes are stored locally in your browser and won't be available on other devices or after clearing browser data.

Loading Quizzes

How to Load a Saved Quiz

  1. Select a quiz from the "Saved Quizzes" dropdown
  2. Click on the quiz name to load it
  3. The quiz will replace the current content in the editor
  4. You can now edit, take, or share the loaded quiz

Deleting Quizzes

How to Delete a Saved Quiz

  1. Select the quiz you want to delete from the dropdown
  2. Click the "Delete" button (trash can icon)
  3. Confirm the deletion when prompted
  4. The quiz will be removed from local storage

Advanced Features

Kwek Kwek Quiz includes several advanced features for power users and collaborative learning.

Peer-to-Peer Sharing

Share quizzes directly with others without needing a server or internet connection.

How it works: Generate a unique code, share it with the recipient, and they can connect directly to receive the quiz.

Offline Mode

Once loaded, quizzes work completely offline without requiring an internet connection.

Perfect for: Studying on the go, areas with poor connectivity, or when you want to minimize data usage.

Progress Tracking

Track your progress through quizzes with visual indicators and detailed summaries.

Features: Progress bars, score tracking, detailed results with explanations.

Troubleshooting

Having issues? Here are some common problems and their solutions.

JSON Parse Error

Symptoms: "Invalid JSON generated" error when using AI features.

Solutions

  1. Check your API key is valid and has Gemini access
  2. Try a simpler prompt - the AI might be confused
  3. Check the console for the raw AI output to see what went wrong
  4. Try again later - the API might be experiencing issues

Image Upload Issues

Symptoms: Image not showing or quiz generation failing with image.

Solutions

  1. Ensure the image is in a supported format (JPEG, PNG, WebP)
  2. Check the image size - very large images might cause issues
  3. Try a different image if the current one isn't working
  4. Make sure your API key has image analysis capabilities

Saving/Loading Issues

Symptoms: Quizzes not saving or loading correctly.

Solutions

  1. Check if local storage is enabled in your browser
  2. Clear browser cache and try again
  3. Try a different browser if the issue persists
  4. Check if you're in private/incognito mode (which might block storage)

Frequently Asked Questions

You only need an internet connection for AI-powered features (quiz generation) and when initially loading the app. Once a quiz is loaded, you can use it completely offline. Saved quizzes and peer-to-peer sharing also work without internet.

Currently, quizzes are stored locally in your browser. You can copy the JSON from the editor and save it manually, or use the peer-to-peer sharing feature to send quizzes to others. Export functionality may be added in future versions.

You can add context to AI-generated quizzes by clicking the "Add Context" button and uploading an image. The AI will use this image to generate relevant questions. For manual quizzes, you can include image URLs in your JSON questions.

Yes! Kwek Kwek Quiz is designed with privacy in mind. All your data is stored locally in your browser - nothing is sent to any servers. The only exception is when using AI features, where your prompt and image (if provided) are sent to Google's API for processing, but this is optional.

Quick Reference

Key Points

  • Quizzes are JSON arrays of question objects
  • Four question types: multiple-choice, true-false, fill-in-the-blank, identification
  • AI features require a Gemini API key
  • All data is stored locally in your browser
  • Works completely offline once loaded

Next Steps

  1. Get your Gemini API key from Google AI Studio
  2. Try generating a sample quiz using the "Sample" button
  3. Explore the AI generation feature with a simple prompt
  4. Save your first quiz and try loading it later
  5. Share a quiz with a friend using peer-to-peer sharing
